mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) reflect increasing consumer spending power, which boosts corporate revenues and earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no positive relationship with month-over-month RSAFS growth; Spearman correlation < 0.1 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 7%, it indicates a sustained labor market recovery, boosting consumer confidence and spending, leading to higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: After UNRATE crosses below 7%, forward 60-day returns are not significantly higher than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.1) over the next 10 crossing events.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is near zero and retail sales are growing, the combination of cheap credit and rising consumer demand strongly boosts equity valuations as discount rates remain low and earnings expectations rise.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS <= 0.25 and RSAFS positive MoM growth
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, forward 20-day returns are not significantly different from zero (mean return non-positive) over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, above-trend industrial production growth signals capacity constraints and potential overheating, leading to a bearish equity market as central banks tighten and profit margins compress.
failure_condition: When INDPRO YoY > 3% in quarters identified as late-cycle by GDP growth above 3% and UNRATE below 5%, the average forward 60-day return is non-negative over all historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the Consumer Price Index reduce real earnings and signal future monetary tightening, causing immediate negative pressure on equity prices over the next week as markets adjust inflation expectations.
failure_condition: The sign of the weekly return is not negatively correlated with month-over-month CPI increase (Spearman rho < -0.05) across all months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in nonfarm payroll employment and retail sales confirm a synchronized economic expansion, lifting corporate earnings and driving positive equity returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM change > 0 and RSAFS MoM change > 0
failure_condition: In months where both conditions hold, the mean 20-day forward return is less than or equal to zero over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts below 800,000 units signals a contraction in residential investment and broader economic weakness, leading to falling equity prices over the next month.
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return is non-negative when HOUST < 800 across all historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-growth environment (annualized GDP growth below 2%), any positive quarterly GDP growth signals the start of a recovery, boosting equity valuations over the following quarter as expectations improve.
failure_condition: In periods where annualized GDP growth is below 2%, positive quarterly GDP growth does not lead to positive 60-day forward returns with statistical significance (t-test p>0.05).
